activities_projects.title: Poem Reading Activity
activities_projects.objective: Understand the basic idea of the poem
steps: Read the poem 'Wind'
Identify who the poet is talking to
Write what the wind does in the poem
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Understands the main idea of the poem

activities_projects.title: Action of the Wind
steps: Write the things the wind breaks or scatters
List at least three examples from the poem
Discuss your answers in class
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Recognizes actions described in the poem

activities_projects.title: Drawing Activity
activities_projects.objective: Visualize the scene in the poem
steps: Imagine strong wind blowing
Draw houses, trees, and papers flying
Label the objects affected by the wind

activities_projects.title: Strong vs Weak Activity
steps: Write examples of weak things mentioned in the poem
Write examples of strong things
Explain how the wind treats them differently
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Understands the difference between strength and weakness

activities_projects.title: Vocabulary Activity
activities_projects.objective: Learn new words from the poem
steps: Find words like frail, crumble, steadfast
Write their meanings
Use them in simple sentences

activities_projects.title: Message of the Poem
activities_projects.objective: Understand the moral of the poem
steps: Read the last part of the poem carefully
Write what the poet advises us to do
Explain the message in 3-4 sentences
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Understands the theme of strength and resilience

activities_projects.title: Reading Aloud Activity
steps: Practice reading the poem
Focus on rhythm and pauses
Read it aloud in class

activities_projects.title: Line Meaning Activity
steps: Write the line 'The wind blows out weak fires'
Explain its meaning
Discuss your explanation with classmates

activities_projects.title: Creative Writing
steps: Write 5 sentences about a windy day
Describe what happens around you
Share your writing in class

activities_projects.title: Life Lesson Activity
activities_projects.objective: Relate the poem to real life
steps: Think about how we can become strong in life
Write two ways to make body and mind strong
Relate it to the poet’s advice
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Connects the poem’s message with real life
